tool_args.code:
import subprocess

notification_message = "✅ Git Backup Succeeded!\n\n**Summary:**\n- Path backed up: /a0/usr\n- Branch: main\n- Status: Successfully pushed to origin/main\n- Timestamp: 2026-04-14 03:00:18\n\nAll changes committed and pushed successfully. No remote conflicts detected."

result = subprocess.run(
    ['python', '/a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py', notification_message, '--title', 'Git Backup Result', '--priority', '3'],
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
    check=False
)
print('Notification sent successfully!')
print('STDOUT:', result.stdout)
if result.stderr:
    print('STDERR:', result.stderr)

## Task:
Execute the git-backup skill by running the backup script at /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py, then use the notify-user skill to send a notification with the backup result.

Steps:
1. Clean up all of the task history if the last run is successful. This is critical to keep our context minimal without losing important details!
2. Run: python /a0/usr/skills/git-backup/scripts/backup.py
3. Capture the output/result
